decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly flood the board with artifact creatures and utilize them as sacrifice fodder for various effects, such as dealing direct damage with Galvanic Blast or drawing cards with Ichor Wellspring and Thoughtcast. The deck also includes cards that synergize well with artifacts, such as Makeshift Munitions and Reckoner's Bargain. By efficiently utilizing artifacts and sacrificing them for value, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays while maintaining card advantage and board presence.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on utilizing a combination of creature sacrifice, land destruction, and artifact generation to overwhelm the opponent. By sacrificing creatures and artifacts, the deck can trigger various effects that disrupt the opponent's strategy and fuel its own board presence. With a mix of removal spells, efficient creatures, and resource manipulation, this deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain tempo throughout the game to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on using Gates as a way to ramp up mana production and control the board through various spells and creatures. With a mix of defensive creatures like Guardian of the Guildpact and Outlaw Medic, along with removal spells like Journey to Nowhere and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to stall the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition. The inclusion of cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Malevolent Rumble also provide additional ways to control the board and outvalue the opponent. The deck's strength lies in its ability to churn out mana and control the battlefield to secure victory.

This Mono Blue Aggro deck focuses on tempo and disruption, utilizing low-cost creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Spellstutter Sprite to apply early pressure while controlling the board with counterspells like Spell Pierce and Counterspell. The deck aims to maintain card advantage through cards like Of One Mind and Ninja of the Deep Hours, while also utilizing efficient card draw options like Snap. The inclusion of Moon-Circuit Hacker and Harrier Strix provides additional evasion and threats, making it a well-rounded aggro deck with a disruptive edge.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Overgrown Battlement and Generous Ent, while utilizing combo pieces like Balustrade Spy and Dread Return to quickly mill through the deck and put key cards into the graveyard. Once the necessary pieces are in place, the deck aims to bring back powerful creatures such as Lotleth Giant and Nyxborn Hydra for a quick win. The deck also includes various cards for card draw and creature tutoring, making it a versatile and efficient combo deck.

The main idea of this deck is to use a combination of cards to create a lock-down situation where your opponent is unable to attack and you can slowly build up your resources. The deck includes various cards that can prevent combat damage, such as Moment's Peace and Tangle, as well as cards that can draw you more cards and replenish your resources, such as Ichor Wellspring and Respite. By stalling the game and slowly gaining advantage, you can eventually overwhelm your opponent with powerful creatures like Crypt Rats and win the game.

This deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent's strategy while simultaneously drawing cards and efficiently using resources. The deck utilizes a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to stay ahead in card advantage and prevent the opponent from executing their game plan. With a mix of efficient threats, disruption, and card filtering, this Dimir Control deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and take control of the game through careful decision-making and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through countering spells and utilizing card draw and graveyard interaction. The goal is to efficiently use counter spells like Counterspell and Spell Pierce to disrupt the opponent's plans, while also using card draw spells like Brainstorm and Deep Analysis to maintain card advantage. The deck also makes use of graveyard interaction with cards like Thought Scour and Cryptic Serpent to provide additional value. Overall, the Mono Blue Terror deck aims to control the game tempo and outmaneuver the opponent through resource management and disruption.

The main idea behind this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures and burn spells to deal direct damage to the opponent's life total. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Gingerbrute and Clockwork Percussionist, along with high-value cards like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent from the very beginning of the game. With a focus on aggressive plays and quick victories, this deck is designed to capitalize on its speed and power to win before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to flood the board with cheap white creatures and use cards like Battle Screech and Guardians' Pledge to overwhelm the opponent quickly. Cards like Raffine's Informant and Leonardo, Big Brother provide additional support and card advantage. Prismatic Strands can be used to protect your creatures or prevent damage. Thraben Inspector and Kor Skyfisher provide additional value with their abilities. The deck also includes some removal in the form of Journey to Nowhere and relic disruption with Relic of Progenitus. Overall, the strategy is to build a fast and aggressive white creature army to outpace the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Goblin Tomb Raider and Gingerbrute, along with direct damage spells like Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt, the aim is to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The deck also includes cards like Clockwork Percussionist and Goblin Bushwhacker to boost the power of creatures and increase the damage output. With a focus on speed and efficiency, this deck aims to win the game as quickly as possible.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize efficient counterspells, removal, and creatures with flash to control the opponent's board and disrupt their game plan. The deck aims to generate value through card draw, tokens, and recursion while maintaining board presence with faeries and ninjas. The inclusion of burn spells provides additional reach and removal options. The key strategy is to outlast the opponent by controlling the game flow, adapting to different threats, and gradually building up advantage to secure a win.

This deck focuses on using cheap and efficient blue spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining pressure with aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Counterspell, and Ponder, the deck aims to always have the right answer at the right time. The inclusion of Lórien Revealed allows for additional card advantage and potentially overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of flyers. Sleep of the Dead provides a finishing blow by tapping down the opponent's blockers.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the Urza lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, Tower) to generate large amounts of mana in order to cast powerful control spells like Deep Analysis and Weather the Storm. By using cards like Crop Rotation and Expedition Map to search for specific lands, the deck aims to assemble the Urzatron as quickly as possible. Additionally, the deck includes card draw engines like Chromatic Sphere and Star, as well as life gain with Fangren Marauder and Weather the Storm to outlast opponents.

The deck focuses on a fast-paced aggressive playstyle, utilizing a mix of control elements and creature abilities to maintain board presence and disrupt opponent's strategies. With a low mana curve, it aims to apply pressure early in the game with efficient creatures like Thraben Inspector and Spellstutter Sprite, while using countermagic like Counterspell and Dispel to protect its board. Card draw and removal spells like Journey to Nowhere supplement its strategy, allowing it to maintain tempo and outpace the opponent in a Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells and removal spells. The deck aims to control the board with efficient removal such as Snuff Out and Cast Down, while also maintaining card advantage with cards like Deep Analysis and Brainstorm. The deck can quickly close out games with powerful threats like Gurmag Angler and Tolarian Terror. It also utilizes cards like Lórien Revealed and Thought Scour to filter through the deck and find key pieces to control the game.
